claim: LLM attention constitutes a zero-sum budget: accuracy drops 13.9-85% as context grows, with performance falling *below closed-book baseline* when relevant information is buried mid-context — empirically validating the thermodynamic budget framing.
evidence: diffray.ai/context-dilution (Dec 2025): 'Research from Stanford, Google, Anthropic, and Meta reveals LLMs suffer predictable performance degradation when context windows contain too much information. GPT-3.5-Turbo accuracy on multi-document QA fell below its closed-book performance when relevant information was placed mid-context with 20 documents present.' Accuracy drops: 13.9% to 85%.
multi_causal_factors: Lost-in-the-Middle positional bias
Softmax crowding at long sequence lengths
Attentional dilution as token count grows

claim: Even with 128K-token windows, positional under-training, encoding saturation, and softmax crowding jointly limit effective context utilization far below nominal capacity, causing effective context to scale sub-linearly with nominal length.
evidence: arXiv:2511.03728 (Fundamental Limits of LLMs, 2026): 'positional under-training, encoding saturation, and softmax crowding jointly limit effective context utilization far below its nominal capacity. Gradient decay at rare positions, vanishing sinusoidal/RoPE overlap, and logarithmic score-margin growth show that effective context scales sub-linearly with nominal length.'
multi_causal_factors: RoPE positional encoding degradation
Gradient vanishing at rare sequence positions
Softmax normalization pressure

claim: The practical saturation threshold for generative code quality is 75% context utilization; sessions at 90%+ generate more raw output but produce lower-quality code with increased frequency of architectural inconsistencies.
evidence: reinteractive.com (Dec 2025, citing Anthropic Sept 2025 engineering blog): 'Claude Code begins context compaction at ~83.5% of context window. Analysis found 75% utilization was optimal quality point; remaining 25% used as working memory. Sessions utilizing 90% generated more raw output but also produced lower-quality code with increased frequency of bugs and inconsistencies in architectural design.'
multi_causal_factors: Context compression reducing working memory
Compaction passes inducing summarization drift
Attention redistribution away from structural anchors

claim: AI codebases become bloated over time as LLMs generate redundant code, causing context loss; large projects exceeding 5,000 lines are particularly prone to structural collapse — the computational analog of topological tearing.
evidence: arXiv:2509.10652 (Vibe Coding for UX Design, Li 2025, cited 4 times): 'AI codebases tended to become bloated over time, generating redundant or unnecessary code that slowed performance and caused context loss. Large projects exceeding 5,000 lines were particularly prone to these issues. P13: It can balloon into something unmanageable.'
multi_causal_factors: Context accumulation beyond saturation threshold
Redundant code inflating structural token count
Loss of architectural coherence under iterative prompting

claim: Under complex long-edit tasks, AI coding assistant satisfaction falls below the midpoint (mean 2.8/5), with participant P9 stating 'I would trash the entire thing and try to start over' — a direct verbal description of the topological tearing failure mode.
evidence: arXiv:2508.07966 (Challenges of AI-Assisted Coding, 2025): participant satisfaction mean = 2.8 (s.d.=1.28) for complex codebases. Short-edit tasks showed higher satisfaction. P9: 'I would trash the entire thing and try to start over. I would never use this.' Unmet functional requirements showed weak correlation with satisfaction (r=-0.02), suggesting structural not functional failure.
multi_causal_factors: Multi-axis prompt complexity exceeding attention payload capacity
Loss of global geometric matrix under iterative mutation
Model regression to baseline template under instruction overload

claim: UI-Bench (2025) — the first rigorous large-scale benchmark of text-to-app tools — reveals systematic quality variance across 10 tools under 30 structured prompts, with 4,000+ expert judgments demonstrating that visual excellence is non-uniformly distributed and highly sensitive to prompt structure.
evidence: arXiv:2508.20410 (UI-Bench, Jung 2025): 'AI text-to-app tools promise high quality applications in minutes, yet no public benchmark had rigorously verified those claims. Spanning 10 tools, 30 prompts, 300 generated sites, and 4,000+ expert judgments, UI-Bench ranks systems with a TrueSkill-derived model yielding calibrated confidence intervals.' v0 excels at UI components; Lovable at full-stack MVPs; Bolt at setup-free development.
multi_causal_factors: Tool-specific attention budget management strategies
Prompt complexity handling varies across model architectures
Expert vs. user perception gap in structural quality assessment

claim: Conversational inertia — strong diagonal attention patterns to previous assistant turns — constrains generative exploration and causes models to over-mimic prior self-generated content, operationalizing the 'Algorithmic Shame' decoherence state.
evidence: arXiv:2602.03664 (Mitigating Conversational Inertia, 2026): 'models exhibit strong diagonal attention to previous corresponding tokens. Context clipping reduces attention to both assistant and diagonal components while slightly increasing attention to system and user inputs. Under good init examples, highest single-position visit percentage is 19%, lower than Full context 21% and Window context 24%, indicating more balanced strategic exploration.'
multi_causal_factors: Diagonal attention to prior assistant turns
Over-exploitation of previous generation patterns
Reduced exploration probability under high-diagonal-attention regime

claim: Summarization drift — where each context compression pass silently discards low-frequency structural details — causes agents to 'remember' a sanitized, generic version of history, precisely the mechanism that produces generic baseline template regression in generative UI.
evidence: arXiv:2603.07670 (Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents, 2026): 'Each compression pass silently discards low-frequency details. After enough passes, the agent remembers a sanitized, generic version of history — precisely the kind of memory that fails on edge cases.' Attentional dilution: 'As more memory content is injected, the model's ability to focus on any single piece degrades — documented in the lost in the middle literature.'
multi_causal_factors: Compression-induced specificity loss
Attentional dilution reducing per-token focus
Generic prior distribution dominating over sparse structural tokens

claim: Lovable's production documentation independently derives the Incremental Isolation Principle: 'Prompt by component, not page — asking it to generate an entire landing page is like dumping a recipe into a blender. Each block should have one clear purpose and structure.'
evidence: docs.lovable.dev (updated Feb 2026): 'Prompt by component, not page. Lovable works best when you build your UI in modular parts — not full pages at once. Asking it to generate an entire landing page is like dumping a recipe into a blender. You'll get something, but it won't be usable.' Lovable best practices: 'Start with a card. Then add a badge. Then add hover states. Each layer builds naturally on the last.'
multi_causal_factors: Modular token budget allocation
Component isolation prevents cross-contamination of structural edits
Sequential layering preserves geometric manifold coherence

claim: AIM Consulting's empirical vibe coding analysis independently validates modular prompting as the key pattern for successful generation: 'Guide it feature by feature. This mirrors how experienced developers structure real-world systems — one component at a time.'
evidence: aimconsulting.com (Nov 2025): 'Rather than asking the AI to build the app, guide it feature by feature: first a data model, then an upload handler, then a business layer. Break tasks into smaller prompts — when you prompt for a small, focused outcome, you get cleaner code. Big prompts often lead to bloated or inconsistent output. Don't hesitate to break the refinement loop when you see no progress; go back to the drawing board and break the failing task into even smaller prompts.'
multi_causal_factors: Focused attention payload reduces multi-axis interference
Smaller prompts improve output verifiability
Iterative refinement loop enables error isolation before geometric propagation

claim: Reddit/vibecoding community empirical practice converges on the Incremental Isolation Principle: structured prompt decomposition into numbered sequential tasks consistently yields 'clear and actionable outputs' vs. bloated or inconsistent outputs from multi-axis prompts.
evidence: r/vibecoding (Feb 2026): 'Build tasks incrementally: (1) Define schema, (2) Set up auth, (3) Create UI for display, (4) Implement feature. This approach consistently yields clear and actionable outputs. Use Chat Mode for brainstorming/debugging, Default Mode for generating actual code. Plan and clarify in Chat, then build in Default.'
multi_causal_factors: Sequential isolation prevents cross-axis attention budget competition
Mode separation (plan vs build) reduces context contamination
Structured prompts guide attention allocation to specific architectural regions

claim: No current text-to-app tool in the UI-Bench evaluation demonstrates reliable multi-axis simultaneous edit success; v0 is explicitly noted as 'not great for more complex apps,' confirming that SSI > 0.08 regime is the observed norm under complex prompt conditions.
evidence: arXiv:2509.10652 (Vibe Coding for UX Design, Li 2025): 'P11: V0 is good for landing pages and quick UI drafts. It's super fast but not great for more complex apps.' AIM Consulting 2025: 'Big prompts often lead to bloated or inconsistent output.' No evidence of reliable four-axis simultaneous edit success in current (Q1 2026) generation of tools.
multi_causal_factors: Tool architecture optimized for single-axis generation
No published multi-axis isolation mechanism in current tool stack
Expert judgment in UI-Bench shows quality variance consistent with SSI prediction

claim: Non-attention architectures (e.g., arXiv:2506.01963) designed to eliminate the QKᵀV matrix and achieve near-linear context scaling represent the strongest candidate for falsifying the topological tearing hypothesis, as they remove the zero-sum attention allocation constraint at the architectural level.
evidence: arXiv:2506.01963 (Non-Attention LLM, 2025): 'This design naturally sidesteps the QKᵀV attention matrix, resulting in significant computational and memory savings for ultra-long sequences. Competitive or superior performance compared to efficient transformer variants. Offers a compelling alternative for tasks requiring massive context windows.' If applied to generative UI, this architecture would remove the thermodynamic budget constraint.
multi_causal_factors: Elimination of quadratic attention scaling
Near-linear context processing removes zero-sum allocation
Falsification requires equivalent SSI performance on ≥4 simultaneous axes
